questions.prompt: The fundamental economic problem of scarcity arises because:
choices: Human wants are unlimited but resources are limited
Money supply is fixed by central banks
Some countries have more resources than others
questions.correctText: Human wants are unlimited but resources are limited

questions.prompt: The opportunity cost of attending college for one year is best measured by:
choices: The value of the next best alternative forgone
The cost of room and board only
questions.correctText: The value of the next best alternative forgone

questions.prompt: A rational decision-maker undertakes an activity as long as:
questions.correctText: Marginal benefit exceeds marginal cost

questions.prompt: A point inside the production possibilities curve (PPC) represents:
choices: Inefficient use of resources or unemployment
questions.correctText: Inefficient use of resources or unemployment

questions.prompt: A bowed-out (concave) PPC reflects:
choices: Increasing opportunity cost as more of one good is produced
questions.correctText: Increasing opportunity cost as more of one good is produced

questions.prompt: Country A can produce 10 cars or 20 computers; Country B can produce 4 cars or 12 computers. Country A has a comparative advantage in:
choices: Cars, because its opportunity cost of a car (2 computers) is lower than B's (3 computers)
Computers, because it can make more of them
Both goods, because it has absolute advantage in both
questions.correctText: Cars, because its opportunity cost of a car (2 computers) is lower than B's (3 computers)

questions.prompt: Mutually beneficial trade between two countries requires the terms of trade to lie:
choices: Between the two countries' opportunity costs
Exactly at one country's opportunity cost
questions.correctText: Between the two countries' opportunity costs

questions.prompt: Which statement is normative rather than positive?
choices: An increase in the minimum wage will raise unemployment among teens
Government ought to provide universal health care
When the price of gas rises, quantity demanded falls
questions.correctText: Government ought to provide universal health care

questions.prompt: In the circular flow model, households supply ___ and firms supply ___.
choices: Factors of production; goods and services
questions.correctText: Factors of production; goods and services

questions.prompt: A command economy answers the basic economic questions primarily through:
questions.correctText: Central government planning

questions.prompt: The law of demand states that, ceteris paribus, as the price of a good rises:
questions.correctText: Quantity demanded falls

questions.prompt: An increase in consumer income shifts the demand curve for an inferior good:
questions.correctText: Leftward

questions.prompt: If coffee and tea are substitutes, a fall in the price of coffee will:
choices: Increase the quantity demanded of tea
questions.correctText: Decrease the demand for tea

questions.prompt: Peanut butter and jelly are complements. If the price of peanut butter rises, the demand for jelly will:
choices: Shift the supply of jelly right
questions.correctText: Decrease

questions.prompt: Which factor would shift the supply curve for pizza to the right?
choices: An increase in the price of cheese
A new tax on pizza producers
A technological improvement in pizza ovens
An expected future increase in pizza prices
questions.correctText: A technological improvement in pizza ovens

questions.prompt: If sellers expect the price of their good to rise next month, current supply will most likely:
questions.correctText: Decrease today

questions.prompt: At a price above equilibrium in a competitive market, there will be:
choices: A shortage that pushes price up
A surplus that pushes price down
Equilibrium with no pressure on price
questions.correctText: A surplus that pushes price down

questions.prompt: A binding price ceiling set below the equilibrium price will create:
choices: A surplus and downward pressure on price
A shortage and possible black markets
An efficient outcome with no deadweight loss
questions.correctText: A shortage and possible black markets

questions.prompt: A minimum wage set above the equilibrium wage in a competitive labor market will:
choices: Create a surplus of labor (unemployment) and deadweight loss
Always raise total wages paid to all workers
questions.correctText: Create a surplus of labor (unemployment) and deadweight loss

questions.prompt: If the price of a good rises 10% and quantity demanded falls 20%, the price elasticity of demand is:
questions.correctText: 2.0, elastic

questions.prompt: Demand tends to be MORE elastic when:
choices: The good takes a small share of the budget
The time horizon is very short
questions.correctText: There are many close substitutes

questions.prompt: If a 5% increase in income causes the quantity demanded of good X to rise 10%, then X is:
questions.correctText: A normal luxury good

questions.prompt: A negative cross-price elasticity of demand between two goods indicates the goods are:
questions.correctText: Complements

questions.prompt: When demand is price elastic, a price decrease will cause total revenue to:
questions.correctText: Increase

questions.prompt: In the short run, at least one factor of production is:
questions.correctText: Fixed

questions.prompt: The law of diminishing marginal returns states that, holding other inputs fixed, additional units of a variable input will eventually cause:
questions.correctText: Marginal product to decline

questions.prompt: Average fixed cost (AFC) as output increases:
questions.correctText: Falls continuously and approaches zero

questions.prompt: Marginal cost (MC) intersects average total cost (ATC) at:
questions.correctText: The minimum of ATC

questions.prompt: Economies of scale exist when, in the long run:
choices: Long-run average cost falls as output rises
Long-run average cost rises as output rises
questions.correctText: Long-run average cost falls as output rises

questions.prompt: Profit is maximized (or loss minimized) where:
questions.correctText: MR = MC

questions.prompt: Which is NOT a characteristic of perfect competition?
questions.correctText: Significant control over price

questions.prompt: For a perfectly competitive firm, the demand curve it faces is:
choices: Perfectly elastic (horizontal) at the market price
questions.correctText: Perfectly elastic (horizontal) at the market price

questions.prompt: A perfectly competitive firm should shut down in the short run if:
choices: Price is below average variable cost
Price is below average total cost
Total revenue is below total fixed cost
questions.correctText: Price is below average variable cost

questions.prompt: In long-run equilibrium for a perfectly competitive industry, economic profit equals:
questions.correctText: Zero

questions.prompt: Productive efficiency in perfect competition is achieved because, in the long run, firms produce where:
questions.correctText: P = minimum ATC

questions.prompt: Allocative efficiency is achieved when:
questions.correctText: P = MC

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a barrier to entry that helps create a monopoly?
questions.correctText: Control of a key resource

questions.prompt: For a single-price monopolist, marginal revenue is:
questions.correctText: Less than price

questions.prompt: A monopolist maximizes profit by choosing output where MR = MC and then sets price:
choices: From the demand curve at that output
questions.correctText: From the demand curve at that output

questions.prompt: Compared with perfect competition, a monopoly typically generates:
choices: Lower output, higher price, and deadweight loss
Zero economic profit in the short run
questions.correctText: Lower output, higher price, and deadweight loss

questions.prompt: First-degree (perfect) price discrimination results in:
choices: Zero consumer surplus and no deadweight loss
questions.correctText: Zero consumer surplus and no deadweight loss

questions.prompt: Which condition is NOT required for successful price discrimination?
choices: Ability to segment buyers by willingness to pay
questions.correctText: Many identical competing firms

questions.prompt: In monopolistic competition, the long-run equilibrium features:
choices: Zero economic profit with price above marginal cost
questions.correctText: Zero economic profit with price above marginal cost

questions.prompt: Monopolistically competitive firms have excess capacity because in the long run they produce where:
choices: Output is less than the output that minimizes ATC
questions.correctText: Output is less than the output that minimizes ATC

questions.prompt: A defining feature of oligopoly is:
choices: Strategic interdependence among a few large firms
questions.correctText: Strategic interdependence among a few large firms

questions.prompt: In a prisoner's dilemma payoff matrix, a dominant strategy is one that:
choices: Is best regardless of what the other player does
Is best only when the rival cooperates
questions.correctText: Is best regardless of what the other player does

questions.prompt: A Nash equilibrium occurs when:
choices: Every firm has the same payoff
No player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing strategy
questions.correctText: No player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing strategy

questions.prompt: OPEC is best described as an example of:
choices: A cartel attempting to coordinate output and price
questions.correctText: A cartel attempting to coordinate output and price

questions.prompt: The demand for labor is called a 'derived demand' because it depends on:
choices: The level of wages workers want
The demand for the goods and services labor produces
The size of the labor union
questions.correctText: The demand for the goods and services labor produces

questions.prompt: Marginal Revenue Product (MRP) of labor equals:
choices: MP_L × MR (or P in perfect competition)
questions.correctText: MP_L × MR (or P in perfect competition)

questions.prompt: In a perfectly competitive labor market, a profit-maximizing firm hires labor up to the point where:
questions.correctText: MRP = MFC (or wage)

questions.prompt: A monopsonist (single buyer of labor) compared to a competitive labor market will:
choices: Pay a higher wage and hire more workers
Pay a lower wage and hire fewer workers
Pay the same wage as competition
questions.correctText: Pay a lower wage and hire fewer workers

questions.prompt: Imposing a minimum wage on a monopsony at the competitive level can:
questions.correctText: Increase both wages and employment

questions.prompt: A firm minimizes the cost of producing a given output when it uses inputs such that:
questions.correctText: MP_L / P_L = MP_K / P_K

questions.prompt: When a factory's pollution imposes uncompensated costs on a community, this is an example of:
questions.correctText: A negative externality

questions.prompt: In the presence of a negative production externality, the unregulated market produces:
choices: Less than the socially optimal quantity
More than the socially optimal quantity, with deadweight loss
questions.correctText: More than the socially optimal quantity, with deadweight loss

questions.prompt: A Pigouvian tax set equal to marginal external cost will:
choices: Internalize the externality and move output toward the socially optimal level
questions.correctText: Internalize the externality and move output toward the socially optimal level

questions.prompt: When vaccinations create benefits beyond those received by the individual, the market will tend to:
choices: Underproduce vaccines relative to the social optimum
Charge a price above the marginal social benefit
questions.correctText: Underproduce vaccines relative to the social optimum

questions.prompt: An appropriate government response to a positive externality is to:
choices: Impose a tax equal to marginal external benefit
Provide a subsidy equal to marginal external benefit
Do nothing because markets are efficient
questions.correctText: Provide a subsidy equal to marginal external benefit

questions.prompt: A public good is characterized by being:
questions.correctText: Non-rival and non-excludable

questions.prompt: The 'free-rider problem' helps explain why private markets tend to:
choices: Produce the efficient quantity of public goods
Produce only private goods that are rivalrous
questions.correctText: Underproduce public goods

questions.prompt: Ocean fisheries open to all fishers illustrate:
choices: A common resource subject to the tragedy of the commons
questions.correctText: A common resource subject to the tragedy of the commons

questions.prompt: On a Lorenz curve diagram, perfect income equality is shown by:
questions.correctText: The 45-degree line of equality

questions.prompt: A Gini coefficient of 0 represents:
questions.correctText: Perfect equality

questions.prompt: A tax in which the average tax rate rises as income rises is:
questions.correctText: Progressive

questions.prompt: A sales tax on basic groceries that takes a larger share of income from lower-income households is best described as:
questions.correctText: Regressive

questions.prompt: When demand is more inelastic than supply, the burden of a per-unit tax falls primarily on:
questions.correctText: Consumers

questions.prompt: Deadweight loss from a per-unit excise tax is generally larger when:
choices: Supply and demand are both highly elastic
Supply and demand are both highly inelastic
questions.correctText: Supply and demand are both highly elastic

questions.prompt: If marginal cost is below average total cost, then ATC must be:
questions.correctText: Falling

questions.prompt: For a perfectly competitive firm in short-run equilibrium, P = MR = MC = $10 and ATC at that output is $8. The firm is earning:
choices: A short-run economic profit of $2 per unit
An economic loss of $2 per unit
questions.correctText: A short-run economic profit of $2 per unit

questions.prompt: Entry of new firms into a perfectly competitive industry causes the market supply curve to:
choices: Shift right, lowering price until economic profit is zero
questions.correctText: Shift right, lowering price until economic profit is zero

questions.prompt: A natural monopoly typically arises because:
choices: Average total cost falls over the entire relevant range of demand due to high fixed costs
questions.correctText: Average total cost falls over the entire relevant range of demand due to high fixed costs

questions.prompt: The kinked demand curve model of oligopoly assumes rivals will:
choices: Match price decreases but ignore price increases
Match price increases but ignore price decreases
questions.correctText: Match price decreases but ignore price increases

questions.prompt: In an unregulated single-price monopoly, allocative inefficiency arises because:
choices: P > MC at the profit-maximizing quantity
P = MC at the profit-maximizing quantity
questions.correctText: P > MC at the profit-maximizing quantity

questions.prompt: If at the profit-maximizing output a monopolistically competitive firm has P = $12, ATC = $14, AVC = $9, the firm should in the short run:
choices: Continue producing because P > AVC and minimize losses
questions.correctText: Continue producing because P > AVC and minimize losses

questions.prompt: An increase in the price of corn (an input) for a tortilla producer will cause the supply of tortillas to:
questions.correctText: Shift left (decrease)

questions.prompt: A producer subsidy on solar panels will most likely:
choices: Shift the supply curve left and raise price
Shift the supply curve right and lower price to consumers
questions.correctText: Shift the supply curve right and lower price to consumers

questions.prompt: Consumer surplus is best defined as:
choices: The price consumers pay minus the cost of production
The difference between what consumers are willing to pay and what they actually pay
questions.correctText: The difference between what consumers are willing to pay and what they actually pay

questions.prompt: Producer surplus equals:
choices: The price received minus the minimum acceptable price (marginal cost) summed over units sold
Total revenue minus total fixed cost
Average revenue minus average variable cost
questions.correctText: The price received minus the minimum acceptable price (marginal cost) summed over units sold

questions.prompt: The price elasticity of supply tends to be HIGHER when:
choices: Firms have less time to adjust production
Inputs are highly specialized and hard to acquire
There is more time for producers to adjust capacity and inputs
The market has only one seller
questions.correctText: There is more time for producers to adjust capacity and inputs
